blocks.text: Of David, when he feigned madness in the presence of Abimelech, who turned him out, and he left.
Cf. 1 Sam. 21.14 ff.
א I bless the Lord at all times; praise of Him is ever in my mouth.
ב I glory in the Lord; let the lowly hear it and rejoice.
ג Exalt the Lord with me; let us extol His name together.
ד I turned to the Lord, and He answered me; He saved me from all my terrors.
ה Men look to Him and are radiant; ו let their faces not be downcast.
ז Here was a lowly man who called, and the Lord listened, and delivered him from all his troubles.
ח The angel of the Lord camps around those who fear Him and rescues them.
ט Taste and see how good the Lord is; happy the man who takes refuge in Him!
י Fear the Lord, you His consecrated ones, for those who fear Him lack nothing.
כ Lions have been reduced to starvation, but those who turn to the Lord shall not lack any good.
ל Come, my sons, listen to me; I will teach you what it is to fear the Lord.
מ Who is the man who is eager for life, who desires years of good fortune?
נ Guard your tongue from evil, your lips from deceitful speech.
ס Shun evil and do good, seek amity and pursue it.
Or “integrity.”
ע The eyes of the Lord are on the righteous, His ears attentive to their cry.
פ The face of the Lord is set against evildoers, to erase their names from the earth.
צ They cry out, and the Lord hears, and saves them from all their troubles.
Viz., the righteous of v. 16.
ק The Lord is close to the brokenhearted; those crushed in spirit He delivers.
ר Though the misfortunes of the righteous be many, the Lord will save him from them all,
ש Keeping all his bones intact, not one of them being broken.
ת One misfortune is the deathblow of the wicked; the foes of the righteous shall be ruined.
The Lord redeems the life of His servants; all who take refuge in Him shall not be ruined.
